content:
This may seem too obvious, but have you rebooted the computer? I very occasionally have had the networking component of my computer crash or start acting weird, and the simplest way of restoring connectivity is to reboot.

Similarly, very occasionally Thunderbird will crash, leaving a phantom process running in the background. The process could be killed with Windows Task Manager or other OS's similar utilities, but often it's most expedient to just reboot.
creator: rednoise

content:
I have never had this happen, nor have I heard of it happening to anyone else. I'm sure it HAS happened, but I don't think it's a common problem with Thunderbird.

My first thought is to blame an anti-virus program, which very well could have, rightly or wrongly, detected a virus in the mail fold file. It might have quarantined the file. Can you check your AV, if any, to see its recent action and if it has moved anything into quarantine?

A moral of this story, which I see you already know, is to backup your Profile regularly!

content:
How exactly are you trying to import the address book? Is it from a file exported from another Thunderbird installation or other program, or are you trying to import it directly from another Thunderbird installation, or is it an on-line address book such as Google Contacts?

If option #1, the answer would be, 'whatever format you had chosen to export it to.' If you have a choice, I recommend VCF (vCard) format.

content:
What is your operating system? What is your Anti-Virus program if any? Is this a POP3 or IMAP account?

The usual suspect for this kind of performance problem is an anti-virus program, which can tie up operations while it scans. This can take a long time, especially for large folders.

Things to try:

- Restart Thunderbird in Troubleshooting Mode - go to <Help | Troubleshoot Mode>. This will temporarily disable all add-ons, in case one of them is misbehaving.

- If you suspect the anti-virus, if using Windows, restart in Safe Mode with Networking. The benefit of this it to temporarily disable the AV. If this cures the problem, set up an exception in the AV for your Thunderbird Profile.
